title: Event Settings
description: Manage your event details, visibility, distances, and registration.

wizard.title: Let's finish setting up your event
wizard.description: Add at least one distance, then publish when you're ready.
wizard.stepZeroDescription: Choose how you want to set up this event. You can switch at any time.

pathHint.ai: AI-assisted setup selected. Use the assistant to draft updates, then edit manually as needed.
pathHint.manual: Manual setup selected. Fill in each section directly and switch to AI any time.

issues.missingEventDate: Add a confirmed start date and time before publishing.
issues.missingEventEndDate: Add an end date to make the event schedule feel complete.
issues.missingEventLocation: Add an event location — city and state, or a map pin.
issues.missingEventDescription: Add a public description so runners know what to expect.
issues.missingHeroImage: Add a cover image to make your event stand out.
issues.missingDistance: Add at least one distance for this edition.
issues.publishMissingDistance: Can't publish yet — add at least one distance first.
issues.publishMissingPricing: Can't publish yet — every distance needs at least one price.
issues.publishActiveAddOnWithoutOptions: Can't publish yet — every active add-on needs at least one active option.
issues.publishLocationNeedsVenueConfirmation: Can't publish yet — the saved location looks like a map match, not a confirmed venue. Go to Basics and confirm the exact address.
issues.publishContentScheduleTruthConflict: Can't publish yet — your event content says the date is unconfirmed, but the schedule is already saved. Update the content to match.
issues.publishContentLocationTruthConflict: Can't publish yet — your event content says the location is unconfirmed, but it's already saved. Update the content to match.
issues.recommendPricingWindows: Consider adding early bird and regular pricing windows.
issues.recommendFaq: Add at least one FAQ to help runners with common questions.
issues.recommendWaivers: Add a waiver so runners can acknowledge race terms at registration.
issues.recommendQuestions: Add registration questions to collect logistics info or preferences.
issues.recommendWebsite: Add website content so runners can review race details.
issues.recommendAddOns: Set up add-ons if you plan to offer extras like shirts or gear.
issues.recommendPolicies: Review your refund, transfer, and deferral policies before going live.

adaptive.title: What to do next
adaptive.description: Prioritized based on your current setup.

wizard.assistantHint: Need help? The setup assistant can draft changes for any step.

steps.distance: Add at least one distance with a price.
steps.publish: Publish to make your event visible.

wizardShell.title: Set up your event step by step
wizardShell.description: Work through each section at your own pace. Come back to anything you skip.

progress.label: Your progress
progress.blockersValue: {publish, plural, =0 {No publish blockers} one {# publish blocker} other {# publish blockers}}{required, plural, =0 {} one { · # required step pending} other { · # required steps pending}}
progress.skippedValue: {count, plural, =0 {No optional steps skipped} one {# optional step skipped} other {# optional steps skipped}}

stepDescriptions.basics: Set the event name, date, location, and the details that everything else builds on.
stepDescriptions.distances: Add the distances runners can register for and configure capacity.
stepDescriptions.pricing: Make sure every distance has a working price before you publish.
stepDescriptions.registration: Set the registration window and control whether sign-ups are open or paused.
stepDescriptions.policies: Set participant waivers and review your refund and transfer policies.
stepDescriptions.content: Write your FAQ and add content for the race page.
stepDescriptions.extras: Add registration questions and optional extras if they add real value.
stepDescriptions.review: Clear any blockers, revisit skipped steps, and control when the event goes live.

footer.description: Work through each step or jump to any section from the sidebar.
footer.optionalHint: You can complete this now or come back to it later.
footer.ready: This step looks good. Continue when you're ready.

placeholder.description: {step} will be available in a future update.

review.readyTitle: Everything looks good. Publish when you're ready.
review.readyDescription: All required setup is complete. Review visibility and publish controls below when you're ready to go live.
review.reviewRecommendedTitle: Required setup is done — a few things worth checking before you publish
review.reviewRecommendedDescription: Required setup is complete. There {count, plural, one {is still # recommended improvement} other {are still # recommended improvements}} worth reviewing before going live.
review.blockedDescription: {publish, plural, =0 {No required publish blockers remain.} one {# required publish blocker remains.} other {# required publish blockers remain.}} {required, plural, =0 {} one {# setup requirement still needs attention.} other {# setup requirements still need attention.}}
review.recheckingDescription: Reviewing your latest event content and setup before showing blockers or readiness.
review.recheckingBody: Checking the latest event state. Current counts will update in a moment.
review.serverAuthority: Publish status reflects the current saved state. The controls below update visibility once all checks pass.
review.savedStateHint: Your changes are saved. Review publish settings now, or exit setup and come back later.
review.savedStateHintWithAssistant: Your changes are saved. Review publish settings now, or close the assistant and come back later from Event settings.
review.noRequiredBlockers: No publish blockers remaining. The recommendations below are still worth a look before going live.
review.recommendationsDescription: These are optional, but they make the experience better for runners.
review.skippedDescription: Optional steps you intentionally skipped stay visible here so they are easy to revisit.
review.noSkipped: No optional steps have been skipped.
review.skippedHelp: Skipped steps won't block publish, but they're worth revisiting for a better runner experience.

assistant.title: Setup assistant
assistant.description: Describe your event and I'll suggest changes you can review and apply.
assistant.contract: I suggest changes, but nothing updates until you apply them.

trust.title: Review before applying
trust.applyDescription: Writes the current proposal to this event step.
trust.applyOnly: Only apply when this proposal looks right for the saved event.
trust.saveDescription: Updates your organizer notes only. It does not change the event fields.
trust.recoveryDescription: The proposal and your notes stay here so you can retry or adjust them without starting over.

recovery.title: This proposal was not applied
recovery.description: Nothing new was saved from that attempt. Review the proposal, adjust your request, or try Apply again when you're ready.
recovery.partialTitle: Some changes were applied before the request failed
recovery.partialDescription: Review the updated event settings before applying anything else. The proposal stayed here so you can confirm what still needs attention.

composer.sectionTitle: What do you need help with in this step?
composer.label: Message
composer.hint: Describe what you want and I'll propose changes. Rough notes are fine.
composer.briefHint: Use this box for what you want to solve right now. Your saved notes stay above.
composer.savedBriefHint: I already have your saved event notes. Add any extra details here — rough is fine.
composer.roughNotesTitle: Paste rough notes. I will turn them into a clear proposal.
composer.roughNotesExample: Examples: "10K at night in Guadalajara, first-timers welcome, music at the finish" or "Need FAQ and site copy, but keep logistics grounded in confirmed details."

inline.title: Assistant for {step}
inline.description: Get help for this step without leaving this setup flow.
inline.markdownHint: Use this to leave the section ready to go.

mobile.stepHint: Get help with {step} without losing your place.

desktop.stepHint: Open the assistant for {step} when you need a wider workspace.
desktop.workspaceDescription: Review proposals, refine your request, and apply changes without cramping the editor.

latestProposal.title: Latest proposal
latestProposal.description: Review the latest suggestion, then keep refining from here.
latestProposal.supportingContextDescription: {count, plural, =0 {See the latest request and response if you need extra context.} one {See the latest request and response plus # earlier message.} other {See the latest request and response plus # earlier messages.}}

handoff.titleMixed: I can keep going here, but the main work fits in {step}
handoff.descriptionCrossStep: This request fits better in {step}, which is where that kind of work lives.
handoff.descriptionMixed: Part of this still touches {currentStep}, but the clearest next step to keep moving is in {step}.

reason.event_description: You're asking about the main event description runners see on the race page.
reason.participant_content: This is more about what runners read than about the core event setup.
reason.faq: This fits better in the FAQ section, where participant questions and answers live.
reason.website_overview: This belongs on the event website, not in this step's settings.
reason.policy: Policies and waivers have their own step where this fits best.
reason.extras: Questions and add-ons have their own step for exactly this.
reason.mixed_content: Your request spans a few content areas — continuing in the right step will keep things clean.
reason.mixed_general: Your request covers more than one setup area. This is the clearest place to continue.

analyzing.title: Reviewing your request
analyzing.description: Checking your notes and the current {step} setup.

grounding.title: Checking the details
grounding.description: Cross-referencing saved event details and any open blockers before drafting {step}.

drafting.title: Drafting your proposal
drafting.description: Putting together a clear, actionable proposal for {step}.

finalizing.title: Getting the proposal ready
finalizing.description: Almost there — preparing the final version for you to review and apply.

event_description.title: Building your event description
event_description.description: Working on the first useful draft while the full proposal is being prepared.

sections.lead: A clear opening paragraph that sets up the race.
sections.confirmed_highlights: Highlights pulled only from confirmed details.
sections.pending_logistics: Logistics that stay pending until you confirm them.

faq.title: Building your FAQ
faq.description: Drafting the first answers around the questions runners are most likely to ask.

sections.event_basics: What the event is, who it's for, and the key details runners need first.
sections.route_and_distances: Route and distance info based only on confirmed details.
sections.registration_and_logistics: Registration and logistics where the current setup supports them.

content_bundle.title: Building FAQ and website overview
content_bundle.description: Drafting the key FAQs and the main website summary together for a stronger first pass.

sections.faq_answers: Short answers to likely questions using only confirmed event details.
sections.website_summary: A main website summary that introduces the event without inventing logistics.
sections.confirmed_boundaries: Clear notes on what's still pending so we don't overpromise.

website_overview.title: Building the website overview
website_overview.description: Framing the site copy around the confirmed event story before expanding it.

sections.hero_positioning: A strong opening that sets the tone without overpromising.
sections.confirmed_experience: Specific race qualities the current setup already supports.
sections.what_to_confirm: Items to mark as tentative until you confirm them.

policy.title: Building the policy draft
policy.description: Organizing the policy into the sections runners expect to see.

sections.core_rule: The main rule or commitment runners need to understand first.
sections.participant_responsibility: What participants agree to, stated plainly.
sections.open_logistics: Operational details that stay open until confirmed.

basics.title: Working on your basics draft
basics.description: Using what I already know to move forward without guessing what's still unconfirmed.

sections.first_pass: Starting with the event name, overall framing, and confirmed details.
sections.confirmed_facts: Using only confirmed details — title, location, timezone, and your notes.
sections.open_points: Anything unclear stays marked as pending rather than guessed.

content.title: Working on the content draft
content.description: The full proposal is on its way — starting with the most useful part now.

sections.first_pass: Starting with the section that matters most for runners.
sections.confirmed_facts: Keeping copy anchored in confirmed event details and existing content.
sections.open_points: Missing details stay pending rather than filled with generic copy.

policies.title: Working on the policy draft
policies.description: Shaping the policy so it reads clearly before the final proposal arrives.

sections.first_pass: Starting with the clearest participant-facing rule or waiver structure.
sections.confirmed_facts: Using only confirmed rules, constraints, and event context.
sections.open_points: Operational details stay open until you confirm them.

review.title: Working on the next step to unblock publish
review.description: The final proposal is coming — already focused on what will help most.

sections.first_pass: Prioritizing the blocker or improvement that makes the biggest difference.
sections.confirmed_facts: Tied to current blockers, your notes, and existing event content.
sections.open_points: Unresolved items stay visible rather than hidden.

generic.title: Working on a first draft
generic.description: The full proposal is on its way — getting something useful together now.

sections.first_pass: Starting with the smallest useful draft this step can support.
sections.confirmed_facts: Using confirmed event details and your saved context.
sections.open_points: Unknowns stay visible so the proposal stays honest.

slow.title: Still working on it
slow.description: This one needs a bit more time — cross-checking your event details before suggesting anything.

verySlow.title: Almost there
verySlow.description: Still going through your notes and confirmed event details to make sure the proposal is accurate.

archive.title: Earlier in this conversation
archive.description: {count, plural, one {# earlier message} other {# earlier messages}}

continuity.eyebrow: Picking up where we left off
continuity.title: I still have your latest proposal from {step}
continuity.description: If you moved here from a suggestion, the last useful exchange stays visible while you continue.

basics.draftFromBrief: Use my notes to start this event.
basics.suggestStrongerPositioning: Make the title and summary stronger.
basics.reviewMostImportantMissingDetails: Tell me the most important basics still missing.

distances.createDistanceLineup: Use my notes to suggest the distances.
distances.suggestCommonDistanceSet: What distance lineup would fit this event?

pricing.draftPricingStructure: Suggest realistic pricing for this event.
pricing.proposeMarketReadyPricing: Suggest prices that feel realistic for this event.
pricing.fixNextPricingBlocker: Tell me which pricing issue to fix first.

policies.writeClearWaiverCopy: Write a waiver that feels easy to understand.
policies.improvePolicyClarity: Review this step and improve the biggest clarity issue.

content.improveContentClarity: Make it clearer and more trustworthy.

review.explainCurrentBlockers: Explain what is still blocking publish.
review.fixNextRequiredStep: Help me with the next required step.
review.improveParticipantCopyBeforePublish: Clean up the race page copy before I publish.

brief.description: Context I use throughout the setup process.
brief.emptyHint: Save your event vision, target audience, tone, and key details once — I'll use them across every step.
brief.inputHint: Edit the notes you want me to keep using throughout setup.
brief.useForStepPrompt: Use my saved event notes to help with {step}.

assistant.placeholder: What do you need help with in this step?

empty.heading: Rough notes are enough to get started.
empty.example: For example: evening 10K in Guadalajara, friendly vibe, live music at the finish, help me write the event summary and tell me what still needs attention.

matched.title: This is the location that will be saved
matched.description: It will be saved to the actual event location field, not just in the public-facing copy.

ambiguous.title: I found a few possible matches
ambiguous.description: I won't set a location yet. Review these options or clarify the place before applying.

choice.title: Which location is correct?
choice.description: Select the right one and I'll use it in the final proposal before you apply changes.
choice.manualClarificationPrompt: None of these is the right place for "{query}". The correct location is:

noMatch.title: I couldn't pin a safe location yet
noMatch.description: I can still help with copy, but the event location still needs to be confirmed separately.

errors.title: Something went wrong
errors.proRequired: The setup assistant is a Pro feature.
errors.disabled: The setup assistant is temporarily unavailable.
errors.readOnlyDescription: You can work through the wizard, but only race directors with edit access can apply proposals.
errors.rateLimited: Too many requests. Wait a moment and try again.
errors.requestFailedHint: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.invalid: That proposal couldn't be applied. Review the step and try again.
errors.retryLater: Couldn't apply that proposal right now. Try again in a moment.

safety.promptInjection: That request doesn't look like event setup. Try asking for help with this step.
safety.policyViolation: That request can't be handled here. Try a more specific event-setup question.

errors.partialApplied: Some changes were applied, but something failed. Please review your event settings.
errors.failed: Couldn't apply changes. Please try again.

visibility.title: Event Visibility
visibility.description: Control who can see your event. Published events appear in the public directory.

errors.missingDistances: Add at least one distance before publishing.
errors.missingPrices: Add at least one price for every distance before publishing.
errors.generic: Couldn't update visibility. Please try again.

registration.title: Registration
registration.description: Pause or reopen registration. Paused events won't accept new sign-ups.

heroImage.title: Cover image
heroImage.description: Shown on your event page and in search results.

errors.invalidType: Please upload a JPG, PNG, WebP, or GIF.

capacity.title: Capacity settings
capacity.description: Choose whether capacity is per distance or shared across the event.
capacity.toggleHelp: When enabled, all distances pull from one shared pool.
capacity.sharedCapacityHelp: Total registrants allowed across all distances.

errors.required: Enter a total capacity to enable shared pool mode.

descriptionField.label: Event description
descriptionField.help: Give runners a clear overview of the event.

details.title: Event Details
details.slugRedirectInfo: Old edition links are automatically forwarded to the new URL.

slugConfirm.title: Update edition URL?
slugConfirm.description: We'll automatically forward your old edition links to the new URL, so existing shares and marketing keep working.

details.startsAtTimeHelp: Use the event timezone so the saved schedule matches the real local start.
details.startTimeRequired: Add the event start date and start time before saving Basics.
details.startTimeInvalid: The selected start date and time could not be saved in this timezone. Choose a valid local time.
details.locationAppliedDescription: Review this field to confirm the exact venue before continuing.

distances.title: Distances
distances.empty: No distances yet. Add at least one to enable registration.
distances.confirmDelete: Delete this distance? This can't be undone.
